sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: position size derived from Y (difference between E[Price at T] and current price) and distributional assumptions to maximize geometric mean. Requires calculable expectation, known price differential Y, and defined holding period T.

source_rule_text: Position sizing should be determined by calculating the optimal f value that maximizes geometric mean, derived from the arithmetic mathematical expectation of the underlying at time T and the difference between that expectation and current price.
